protocols.vedic_correspondence: Mangal — element fire (agni), direction south, day Tuesday, mantra "Om Angarakaya Namaha"
protocols.energy_is_for: Cutting through what no longer serves; defending what matters.

steps.title: Name
steps.body: "I am feeling anger." Say it out loud or in your head. Do not justify it. Do not story it.

steps.title: Locate
steps.body:
Where is it in your body?

Put a hand on the strongest location.

steps.title: Breathe
steps.body: Mars breath: inhale 4 counts through the nose. Hold 2. Exhale 6 through the mouth with sound (audible "haaa"). Three rounds.

steps.title: Reframe
steps.body: This is fuel, not fault. Anger means a boundary was crossed or a value was violated. The question is not "how do I stop being angry" — it's "what is this anger asking me to protect?"

steps.title: Route
steps.body: Pick one:

options.action: Ask the clarifying question. "Help me understand what you meant by ___."
Write the boundary in one sentence. "From now on, I do not ___."
Identify the unmet need. "What did I want that I didn't get?"

steps.title: Optional ritual
steps.body: Light a red candle. Or splash cold water on wrists. Or do 10 push-ups. Mars wants discharge through the body, not the mouth.

protocols.vedic_correspondence: Chandra — element water (jala), direction northwest, day Monday, mantra "Om Chandraya Namaha"
protocols.energy_is_for: Receiving, releasing, integrating what the day did not let you feel.

steps.title: Name
steps.body: "I am feeling sadness." Say it. Do not minimize it. Do not call it tired, fine, or off.

steps.title: Locate
steps.body:
Where is it in your body?

Put a hand on the strongest location. Let it rest there.
options: Hollow in the chest or stomach

steps.title: Breathe
steps.body: Moon breath: inhale 6 counts through the nose. Exhale 8 counts through the nose, longer than the inhale. Soft, no force. Two rounds.

steps.title: Reframe
steps.body: Sadness is the body metabolizing a loss — small or large, named or unnamed. It is not a problem to fix. It is water passing through. Your only job is to let it pass.

steps.title: Route
steps.body: Pick one:

options.action: Let the tears come if they are there. One long sigh. A slow 5-minute walk, no phone.
Drink a full glass slowly. Or splash your face. Or step into the shower. If you can reach an ocean, lake, or river — go.
One sentence. "I am sad because ___ is not what I wanted it to be."

steps.title: Optional ritual
steps.body: Light a white or silver candle. Place a bowl of water near you. Look at the moon if it is night. Chandra does not ask you to be okay — only to be present with what is.

do_not: Call the person you broke up with.

protocols.vedic_correspondence: Shani — element earth and air, direction west, day Saturday, mantra "Om Shanaye Namaha"
protocols.energy_is_for: Structural caution; respect for limits; long-term wisdom that the lizard brain mistakes for danger now.

steps.title: Name
steps.body: "I am feeling fear." Not anxious. Not stressed. Fear. The precise word is the first weight off.

steps.title: Locate
steps.body:
Where is it in your body?

Put a hand on the strongest location. Feet flat on the floor.
options: Tightness in the gut, shallow breath
Buzzing in the chest, racing heart

steps.title: Breathe
steps.body: Saturn breath: box breath. Inhale 4. Hold 4. Exhale 4. Hold 4. Two rounds. Mouth closed throughout.

steps.title: Reframe
steps.body: Fear is your nervous system asking for structure, not escape. It is information about a future you have not built scaffolding for yet. The task is to give it scaffolding.

steps.title: Route
steps.body:
Two sentences, written down. Phone notes, paper, whatever is closest.

If you cannot verify anything in 24 hours, the fear is about something further out. Schedule a 15-minute block this week to plan it. Then close the loop.

options.action: "The worst that can happen is ___." Specific. One sentence.
"In the next 24 hours I can verify ___." One concrete check — a message, a search, a number, a person.

steps.title: Optional ritual
steps.body: Light a dark blue or black candle. Touch the earth — barefoot on floor, hand on stone, palm on a tree. Shani is slow on purpose. He is asking you to slow down with him.

protocols.vedic_correspondence: Shukra — element water (jala), direction southeast, day Friday, mantra "Om Shukraya Namaha"
protocols.energy_is_for: Pointing at what is meaningful; calibrating your values; showing you where you want to grow toward.

steps.title: Name
steps.body: "I am feeling desire." Or wanting. Or pull. Name the object: "I want ___." Out loud is better.

steps.title: Locate
steps.body:
Where is it in your body?

Put a hand on the chest, palm flat.
options: Warmth in the chest, heart racing
Pull in the lower belly, the hips

steps.title: Breathe
steps.body: Heart breath: hand on chest. Inhale 5 through the nose, into the hand. Exhale 5 through the nose. Slow, even. Three rounds. Let the hand feel the rise and fall.

steps.title: Reframe
steps.body: Desire is a compass needle, not a command. It points. It does not say "now." The signal matters. The timing is yours.

steps.title: Route
steps.body:
Distinguish craving from longing.

Write one sentence: "This is craving / longing because ___."

Then: **wait 24 hours before acting.** If at hour 24 it is still there, it is longing. Act with care. If it dissolved, it was craving. Notice what it was actually pointing at.

options.action: Craving is away from something — a feeling you do not want to have. Boredom, loneliness, smallness. The object is incidental.
Longing is toward something — a quality of life you want more of. Beauty, intimacy, mastery, meaning. The object is a doorway.

steps.title: Optional ritual
steps.body: Light a white or pink candle. Eat something sweet, slowly. Look at something beautiful for 60 seconds — art, a face, a flower, the sky. Shukra teaches that beauty is not optional; it is how the soul calibrates.

protocols.vedic_correspondence: Budha — element earth and air, direction north, day Wednesday, mantra "Om Budhaya Namaha"
protocols.energy_is_for: Information that has not sorted yet. The mind is mid-process, not broken.

steps.title: Name
steps.body: "I am feeling confusion." Not stupid. Not behind. Confused. The data is in; the pattern is not.

steps.title: Locate
steps.body:
Where is it in your body?

Put a hand on the forehead or the back of the neck.
options: Static behind the eyes, fog in the forehead
Restlessness in the hands, the jaw

steps.title: Breathe
steps.body: Mercury breath: equal breath. Inhale 4 through the nose. Exhale 4 through the nose. Mouth closed. Four rounds. Eyes soft or closed.

steps.title: Reframe
steps.body: Confusion is not the absence of thought. It is too much thought, unsorted. The fix is not more input. The fix is structure.

steps.title: Route
steps.body:
Three sentences. Write them down.

If the third sentence is answerable — go find the answer. If it is not, the decision is not ready yet. That is a fact, not a failure.

options.action: One thing that is genuinely unclear.

options.condition: What I'd need to know to decide

steps.title: Optional ritual
steps.body: Light a green candle. Pick up a pen and write longhand for two minutes — anything, do not lift the pen. Or take a 10-minute walk without your phone. Budha sorts in motion and in ink, not in the scroll.
